DynamicHeader Union Reference
[Low-Level UDN Support (arch/udn.h)]

Final routing destinations for dynamic messages. More...


Data Structures

struct  _bits
 Useful for setting route parameters. More...

Data Fields

unsigned int word
 For passing to udn0_send() and the like.
struct DynamicHeader::_bits bits
 Useful for setting route parameters.

Detailed Description

Final routing destinations for dynamic messages.

The first word of a message on a dynamic network is a header bearing an (x, y) tile destination and a "final route". The final route determines whether the message should go to the tile CPU or head in a cardinal direction of the edge of the chip. These bitfields are unioned with a 32-bit "int" type so that a DynamicHeader can be passed to udn0_send() or other similar routines.

Examples:

tmc/multi_tile/multi_tile.c.

Field Documentation

unsigned int DynamicHeader::word

For passing to udn0_send() and the like.

struct DynamicHeader::_bits DynamicHeader::bits

Useful for setting route parameters.
